channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US and Iran Agree on Temporary Two-Week Ceasefire Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: After more than five weeks of intense conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran, a temporary two-week ceasefire has been agreed. US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth stated that Iran requested the ceasef...

item.title: 75-year-old Pune doctor loses Rs 12 crore in investment scam
item.description: A 75-year-old doctor from Pune has become the victim of an elaborate investment scam, resulting in a loss of Rs 12 crore. According to the police, the doctor was misled by fraudulent individuals posin...

item.title: Iran accuses US of violating ceasefire terms amid continued strikes in Lebanon and closure of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran has accused the United States of breaching a recently agreed ceasefire, claiming that Israel’s attacks on Lebanon, drones entering Iranian airspace, and Washington’s refusal to allow uranium enri...

item.title: Montreal hospital explores virtual reality concerts to reduce stress and pain in patients
item.description: St. Mary’s Hospital in Montreal is pioneering a new approach to health care that combines virtual reality (VR) and music to help patients relax, escape stress, and potentially accelerate healing. The ...

item.title: Caloocan Diocese Issues Apology Over Live Pigeon Used in Easter Salubong Ritual in Malabon
item.description: The Diocese of Caloocan has issued a public apology for an Easter Sunday salubong ritual held at the Diocesan Shrine and Parish of the Immaculate Conception in Malabon. During the event, a live pigeon...

item.title: Rivers Police Hold Community Programmes to Mark 2026 National Police Day
item.description: Today, the Rivers State Police Command don mark 2026 National Police Day with plenty community-oriented activities to rebuild trust and strengthen partnership with residents. CSP Grace Iringe-Koko yan...

item.title: Sam Thaiday suggests Ben Te'o could return to the Dolphins amid pressure on Kristian Woolf
item.description: NRL veteran Sam Thaiday has suggested that former Broncos assistant Ben Te'o could make a return to the Dolphins, signalling potential changes as scrutiny grows around current coach Kristian Woolf. Th...

item.title: Iran Requires Bitcoin Payments for Oil Tankers in Strait of Hormuz During Ceasefire
item.description: Iran has announced that it will require shipping companies to pay tolls in cryptocurrency, specifically Bitcoin, for any oil tankers passing through the Strait of Hormuz during the two-week ceasefire....

item.title: Golf Canada CEO talks up Canadians' chances at the Masters
item.description: Laurence Applebaum, the CEO of Golf Canada, has expressed optimism about the chances of Canadian golfers at the 2023 Masters. With players like Corey Conners, Nick Taylor, and Mike Weir competing, App...

item.title: COMCIN and NEAT Microcredit Launch N100m Loan Facility for SMEs Nationwide
item.description: The Coalition of Microlending and Cooperative Institutions in Nigeria (COMCIN) don partner with NEAT Microcredit to start one N100 million joint loan scheme wey go help small and medium-scale enterpri...

item.title: Canva accelerates AI expansion through acquisitions of Simtheory and Ortto
item.description: Canva, the Sydney-based design platform, has acquired two Australian technology companies, Simtheory and Ortto, as part of its strategy to evolve into a comprehensive AI-powered productivity suite. Si...

item.title: Iran-US Ceasefire Faces Challenges as Israel Strikes Lebanon
item.description: The ongoing conflict in West Asia has entered a delicate phase following a two-week ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran. U.S. President Donald Trump announced the temporary truce, c...

item.title: Supreme Court discusses misuse of PILs while revisiting Sabarimala case
item.description: The Supreme Court on Wednesday reflected on how the approach toward Public Interest Litigations (PILs) has evolved, noting that the Sabarimala petition filed in 2006 might not be entertained under cur...

item.title: Fuel supply disruption reported in parts of Mombasa
item.description: Several petrol stations in Mombasa have recently faced a shortage of fuel, leaving many motorists stranded and worried about rising transport costs. According to local fuel dealers, the shortage start...

item.title: Maritime businesses experience increased theft despite conflicting official statistics
item.description: Businesses across the Maritime provinces of Canada are reporting an uptick in theft, despite conflicting reports from law enforcement and official statistics. While some police data suggest theft rate...

item.title: Nationwide fuel shortage disrupts transport and supply chains
item.description: Kenya has once again been hit by a serious fuel shortage, with petrol stations in many towns and cities running dry. Long queues have formed in Nairobi, Mombasa, Eldoret, and Kisumu as motorists scram...

item.title: Australian fuel prices expected to remain high for months despite US-Iran ceasefire and Strait of Hormuz reopening
item.description: Australians have been advised not to anticipate an immediate reduction in petrol and diesel prices following progress in a ceasefire between the United States and Iran. The agreement includes a two-we...

item.title: Ben Delo Donates £4m to Reform UK Amidst Political Support for Farage
item.description: Ben Delo, a British cryptocurrency billionaire, has donated £4 million to Reform UK, the political party led by Nigel Farage. Delo, who was previously convicted in the US for violating banking laws re...

item.title: Potential Reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and Its Global Economic Implications
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global oil and trade, may soon reopen following a conditional ceasefire announced between the US and Iran. Since 28 February, Iran's military control ov...

item.title: VP Duterte honors bravery in safeguarding Philippine sovereignty and rule of law
item.description: During the commemoration of Araw ng Kagitingan, Vice President Sara Duterte paid tribute to the courageous soldiers who defended Bataan during World War II. She emphasized that their sacrifices were n...
